Residential Stucco in Bastrop County, TX

Bastrop County offers a unique blend of pine forests, Colorado River bottomland, and open prairie — a setting that demands exterior finishes built for moisture, heat, and fire safety. Star Stucco of Austin installs residential stucco systems that protect and enhance homes throughout Bastrop, Elgin, Smithville, and the Lost Pines region.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Stucco Built for Bastrop County Conditions

Bastrop County is distinct from much of Central Texas thanks to the Lost Pines, an isolated pocket of loblolly pines that defines the landscape east of Austin. The wildfires of 2011 and beyond underscored the importance of fire-resistant building materials in this region, and stucco has become an increasingly popular choice for homeowners who want both beauty and safety.

The soils across Bastrop County vary from sandy loam in the pine forest areas to heavy black clay along the Colorado River corridor and near Elgin. This variation means no single approach works everywhere — we assess soil and site conditions at each property before specifying the stucco system to ensure the best long-term performance.

Whether you are rebuilding after wildfire, constructing a new home in one of Bastrop's growing neighborhoods, or updating an older property in Smithville, we bring the right materials and techniques for your specific location.

What's Included With Our Residential Stucco in Bastrop County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Property Assessment

We evaluate your home's exterior walls, fire exposure risk, soil type, and existing finishes to design a stucco system tailored to your Bastrop County property.

Surface Preparation

Substrates are cleaned, damaged areas repaired, and a proper weather-resistive barrier is installed to protect against moisture from Bastrop County's heavy spring rains.

Lath & Scratch Coat

Corrosion-resistant metal lath is installed and a scratch coat applied with proper keying. Control joints are placed based on the home's geometry and soil conditions.

Brown Coat Leveling

The brown coat establishes a smooth, plumb surface. We check alignment and thickness at this stage to guarantee a clean finish application.

Finish Texture & Color

Your chosen texture is applied by experienced finishers. We use integral color or acrylic coatings for a finish that resists UV fade and stays vibrant.

Completion & Documentation

We clean the job site, walk the finished work with you, and provide written warranty information and maintenance tips specific to Bastrop County conditions.

Common Residential Stucco Needs in Bastrop County

Bastrop County homeowners value stucco for its fire resistance, durability, and versatility across the county's varied landscape.

Fire-Resistant New Construction

After the devastating Bastrop County wildfires, many homeowners are choosing stucco for new construction because it is noncombustible and offers real protection in wildfire-prone pine forest areas.

Full Home Re-Stucco

Older homes in Bastrop and Smithville with worn or damaged exteriors benefit from a complete re-stucco that restores weather protection and refreshes the look.

Stucco for Rural Properties

Homes on large lots and rural acreage throughout Bastrop County face high wind and sun exposure. Our stucco systems are applied to handle these open-site conditions.

Texture Matching for Home Additions

Adding square footage to a stucco home means the new section must blend perfectly with the original. We match texture, color, and profile for a seamless result.

Exterior Upgrades in Growing Communities

As Bastrop and Elgin attract new residents, homeowners updating their properties choose stucco to stand out and increase home value in a rising market.

Why Choose Us for Residential Stucco in Bastrop County?

  • Understanding of Bastrop County's varied soils, from sandy loam to heavy clay
  • Fire-resistant stucco systems suited for the Lost Pines wildfire interface zone
  • Straightforward assessments with no unnecessary upselling
  • Tidy crews who take care around wooded lots and rural properties
  • UV-stable, weather-resistant finishes that hold up in Central Texas conditions
  • Free estimates for homeowners throughout Bastrop County

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Bastrop County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Residential Stucco Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Request an Estimate

Reach out at (512) 706-9699 or online. We will arrange a time to visit your Bastrop County property for a free evaluation.

2

Evaluate Your Home

Our estimator reviews your walls, site conditions, fire exposure, and soil type to build a stucco plan specific to your property.

3

Receive Your Proposal

A written proposal covers materials, scope, timeline, and total cost. We answer all your questions before you make a decision.

4

Stucco Installation

We complete the work as specified, keep the site clean, and walk the finished project with you before wrapping up.
